entities.name: Job
entities.definition: One /imagine roll through to its terminal state (done | failed). Identified by job_id.

entities.name: Asset
entities.definition: A named output the user is generating (e.g., 'bird', 'clue_a'). Identified by asset_id.

entities.name: Bridge
entities.definition: The long-running Flask + discord.py-self daemon process. Operated through HTTP.

entities.name: Backend
entities.definition: The pluggable backend implementing ImageGenerationBackend. v0.1 has one (MidjourneyDiscordBackend).

invariants.note: Every tool call resolves to one of the two terminal tags.
Terminal — no further job-state tags carry the same job_id.
Terminal — no further job-state tags carry the same job_id.

rules.note: Either CONFIG_VALIDATED follows CASCADE_INIT or the process exited via CONFIG_VALIDATION_FAILED before.
Grid received must follow grid matched for the same job_id.
Slot N's UPSCALE_RECEIVED follows its UPSCALE_REQUESTED.
No further job-category tags carry the same job_id after a terminal.

operators.name: MidjourneyDiscordBackend
operators.consumes: HTTP calls from the MCP server, CLI, or library consumers

project_overrides.name: validator-extras
project_overrides.note: Payload fields not declared in the schema raise at emit; production may relax via CASCADE_STRICT_SIGNALS=false.

project_overrides.name: view-payload-universal
project_overrides.note: No UI in cascade-img; the view category does not exist.

tags.name: CASCADE_INIT
tags.note: Daemon process started; config loaded; backend selected.

tags.name: CONFIG_VALIDATED
tags.note: Config.from_env() returned successfully. has_guild_id is bool — DM-only deployments may legitimately have it false.

tags.name: CONFIG_VALIDATION_FAILED
tags.note: MissingEnvError raised during Config.from_env(). Daemon will not start.

tags.name: BRIDGE_HEALTHY
tags.note: GET /health returned. Use discord_ready to gate /imagine submissions.

tags.name: DISCORD_CONNECTED
tags.note: discord.py-self WebSocket handshake completed; on_ready fired.

tags.name: DISCORD_DISCONNECTED
tags.note: WebSocket connection dropped. reason is 'gateway_close' (normal disconnect with a close code), 'exception' (transport error raised), or 'on_disconnect' (discord.py-self lifecycle event). _ready is cleared; new /imagine calls return 503 DISCORD_NOT_READY until reconnect.

tags.name: DISCORD_RECONNECTING
tags.note: Outer reconnect loop sleeping before next client.start(). attempt is 1-indexed; backoff_seconds is the wait before retry. Capped at 60s; loop runs until success or terminal auth failure.

tags.name: DISCORD_RECONNECT_FAILED
tags.note: Reconnect loop gave up. reason is 'auth' (token rejected — terminal; operator must rotate token) or 'shutdown' (process exiting). Daemon stays up but /imagine returns DISCORD_NOT_READY indefinitely until restart.

tags.name: IMAGINE_FIRED
tags.note: POST /imagine accepted by the bridge and Discord interaction sent. upscale is null|'1'|'2'|'3'|'4'|'all'.

tags.name: JOB_SUBMIT_TIMEOUT
tags.note: Discord interaction POST did not return within the bridge's interaction-call timeout, but MJ may still have processed the imagine. Job stays in PENDING_GRID and transitions to SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED; the daemon continues to watch for a matching grid message. /imagine returned 202 with job_id; client should /wait or /status to learn the outcome rather than retry blindly (a retry will bill MJ twice if the original did process).

tags.name: GRID_MATCHED
tags.note: MJ message matched to job. match_path is 'pending' (first-touch on MJ's initial prompt-echo) or 'progress_fallback' (matched after the job was already in PROGRESS, which happens when MJ posts the final grid as a new message instead of editing the original).

tags.name: GRID_RECEIVED
tags.note: Grid PNG downloaded to disk.

tags.name: OUTPUT_PATH_COLLISION
tags.note: Intended output path already existed on disk (another concurrent job with the same asset_id wrote there first). The daemon wrote to actual_path instead — request_token suffix appended to disambiguate. kind is 'grid' or 'upscale'. The asset_id contract is preserved (the artifact exists) but the operator should investigate why two jobs shared an asset_id.

tags.name: UPSCALE_REQUESTED
tags.note: Upscale button (U1-U4) pressed via Discord interaction. slot is 1-4.

tags.name: UPSCALE_PRESS_FAILED
tags.note: Single-slot button press failed during upscale='all'. Other slots may still succeed and land — the job stays UPSCALING. error_code is 'HTTP_<status>', 'TimeoutError', or the underlying exception class. If every requested slot fails, the job transitions to JOB_FAILED with UPSCALE_ALL_BUTTONS_FAILED.

tags.name: UPSCALE_RECEIVED
tags.note: Single upscale PNG downloaded to disk.

tags.name: JOB_COMPLETED
tags.note: Job hit DONE — all expected artifacts on disk.

tags.name: JOB_FAILED
tags.note: Job hit FAILED. error_code is a stable string and the evidence-constraints enum is canonical (validated at emit). Values: DISCORD_400_OUTDATED, DISCORD_400_UNKNOWN_CHANNEL, DISCORD_401, DISCORD_HTTP_ERROR (any other non-2xx from the Discord interaction; the status is in error_message), DISCORD_NOT_READY (WebSocket dead at submit time), GRID_DOWNLOAD_FAILED, UPSCALE_DOWNLOAD_FAILED, MJ_UUID_MISSING, SUBMIT_FAILED, UPSCALE_BUTTON_FAILED (single-slot upscale press failed and was the only slot), UPSCALE_ALL_BUTTONS_FAILED (every requested upscale='all' slot's press failed), RESUBMIT_REQUIRED (a pre-grid job was failed on daemon-restart rehydration because MJ's processing is unconfirmable across a restart; the operator should re-submit and verify rather than risk a double-bill).

tags.name: BACKEND_HTTP_CALLED
tags.note: MidjourneyDiscordBackend made an HTTP call to the bridge daemon. Useful for grading the client-side, not just the daemon.

tags.name: CROP_QUADRANT
tags.note: crop_quadrant() emitted a cropped image. quadrant=0 means the whole image was returned (single-upscale passthrough), 1-4 means a grid quadrant. w/h are the OUTPUT dimensions.

tags.name: ALPHA_KEY_APPLIED
tags.note: alpha_key_corners() processed an image. bg_r/bg_g/bg_b are the four-corner average. keyed_count/total_count gives the proportion of pixels keyed transparent — typical sprite values are 30-70%. A keyed ratio outside that band suggests tolerance is wrong (too tight = halo, too loose = sprite eaten).

tags.name: ASSET_PROMOTED
tags.note: promote() copied a curated asset to the consumer's asset tree.

tags.name: CONTACT_SHEET_RENDERED
tags.note: contact_sheet() composited a grid's panels (with optional index/score labels) into one annotated image — a far better single input for vision-model selection than four separate reads. tiles is the number of panels placed; w/h are the output sheet dimensions.

tags.name: AUTO_TRIM_APPLIED
tags.note: auto_trim() cropped an image to its content bounding box. bbox is [left, top, right, bottom] in source pixels; w/h are the trimmed output dimensions. A bbox spanning the whole image means nothing was trimmed.

tags.name: PALETTE_QUANTIZED
tags.note: palette_quantize() reduced an image to a fixed palette for the limited-palette look. method is 'median_cut', 'maximum_coverage', or 'octree'; n_colors is the requested palette size.

tags.name: SPRITE_SHEET_PACKED
tags.note: sprite_sheet() packed N curated sprites into one atlas plus a JSON frame map written alongside it. layout is 'grid', 'row', or 'column'; count is the number of frames; w/h are the atlas dimensions.

tags.name: GRID_SCORED
tags.note: score_grid() ranked the four quadrants of a 2x2 grid on sharpness/contrast/edge-density so an agent picks on evidence instead of a blind quadrant int. tiles is the number scored (4); top_slot (1-4) is the highest composite score. Advisory — the agent still confirms with vision.

tags.name: PROMPT_COMPOSED
tags.note: PromptComposer.compose() emitted a backend-ready prompt string. prompt_parts_used lists which of the optional parts were included (moodboard, sref, stylize, oref, ow).

tags.name: PROMPT_LOGGED
tags.note: PromptLog.append() wrote a record. agent_decision is one of '', 'promote', 'reroll', 'escalate'. Records read back via PromptLog.read() are the agent's working memory across loop iterations.

tags.name: MCP_SERVER_STARTED
tags.note: cascade-mcp process started. transport is 'stdio' (default — Claude Desktop / Cursor) or 'http'.

tags.name: MCP_TOOL_CALLED
tags.note: An MCP tool invocation began. tool is one of the names in the MCP_TOOL_CALLED|MCP_TOOL_COMPLETED|MCP_TOOL_FAILED evidence-constraint enum (the authoritative list): compose_prompt, imagine, wait, status, bridge_health, crop_grid, alpha_key, promote, log_append, read_prompt_log, contact_sheet, auto_trim, palette_quantize, sprite_sheet, score_grid, mj_action.

tags.name: MCP_TOOL_COMPLETED
tags.note: Tool returned successfully; duration_ms is wall-clock from MCP_TOOL_CALLED.

tags.name: MCP_TOOL_FAILED
tags.note: Tool raised. error_code is the exception class name or the structured `code` attribute if the exception carried one.

tags.name: BRIDGE_CHECKENV_RAN
tags.note: cascade-mj-bridge --check-env completed. error_code is empty string on ok, otherwise the structured Config error code.

tags.name: BRIDGE_DOCTOR_RAN
tags.note: cascade-mj-bridge --doctor completed. Pre-flight ladder: env + discord reachability + mcp_server import + discord.py-self import.

tags.name: CLI_ROLL_STARTED
tags.note: cascade-mj CLI accepted an asset_id and began the roll. upscale is 'grid' (default, no upscale), '1'-'4' (single), or 'all'.

tags.name: CLI_ROLL_COMPLETED
tags.note: cascade-mj CLI returned. status is 'done', 'failed', 'dry_run', or 'unknown'.

tags.name: CLI_ROLL_FAILED
tags.note: cascade-mj CLI exited with a structured error. error_code is one of UNKNOWN_ASSET_ID, FileNotFoundError, ValueError (registry malformed), or any backend exception class name.

tags.name: BRIDGE_SHUTDOWN
tags.note: Bridge daemon shutting down. reason is 'signal' (SIGINT/SIGTERM), 'config_error' (failed startup), or 'exception' (unhandled).

tags.name: JOB_EVICTED
tags.note: Job evicted from the in-memory JOBS dict. reason is 'lru_capacity' (oldest evicted to stay under cap) or 'terminal_age_ttl' (DONE/FAILED job exceeded retention). age_seconds is wall-clock since job created_at.

tags.name: MCP_SERVER_STOPPED
tags.note: MCP server exited. reason is 'signal' or 'exception'.

tags.name: JOB_STORE_INITIALIZED
tags.note: The persistent job store opened at daemon startup. mode is 'sqlite' (CASCADE_JOB_DB path; survives restart) or 'memory' (':memory:' — ephemeral, opted in for tests/short runs). path is the db path or ':memory:'.

tags.name: JOB_STORE_REHYDRATED
tags.note: Non-terminal jobs restored from the persistent store into the in-memory JOBS map at startup, so a bridge restart resumes tracking in-flight jobs instead of dropping them. count is the number rehydrated.

tags.name: MJ_ACTION_REQUESTED
tags.note: A response-message button was pressed on a completed job's upscaled image (vary/zoom/pan/upscale-variant/animate/favorite). The bridge reads the live custom_id off the message and presses it — never hardcoded. action is the requested action name; message_id is the message the button lives on.

tags.name: MJ_ACTION_FAILED
tags.note: A response-message action could not be pressed. error_code is NO_UPSCALED_IMAGE (the job has no upscaled image to act on), BUTTON_NOT_FOUND (no matching button on the live message), DISCORD_NOT_READY, or HTTP_<status>.

tags.name: MJ_DERIVED_RECEIVED
tags.note: A derived result produced by an mj_action press landed and was downloaded to the parent asset. Routed by Discord message_reference == the SOLO upscaled-image message id (upscale_message_id) — the only signal present on every family; recency is unsafe because the MJ channel is shared. action_kind is the family classified from MJ's content suffix (variation/zoom/pan/upscale/animation). animation lands as an ANIMATED WEBP (content_type image/webp, NOT video/mp4). mj_uuid is the NEW MJ job uuid minted for the derived result. favorite presses produce no artifact and emit nothing here.

tags.name: MJ_DERIVED_FAILED
tags.note: A derived result was matched to its parent job but could not be downloaded. The parent job stays DONE — a derived result is additive, so a download failure is an incident on the derived artifact, not a job failure. error_code is the exception class or HTTP status.
